raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2695 requires the Department of Justice to align its records and data with the federal National Incident-Based reporting system.",
    "AB 2695 will assist law enforcement in understanding where criminal incidents related to Missing and Murdered Indigenous People (MMIP) are occurring.",
    "California ranks fifth in the nation in unresolved MMIP cases.",
    "For the first time in California, law enforcement agencies will be required to collect data on crimes occurring on Indian lands.",
    "Collecting data on crimes occurring on Indian lands will help develop data-driven strategies to address the MMIP crisis in California.",
    "A National Institute of Justice 2016 report found that 84% of American Indian/Alaska Native women experience violence in their lifetimes.",
    "67% of American Indian/Alaska Native women reported being concerned for their own safety.",
    "41% of American Indian/Alaska Native women reported having been physically injured from violence by intimate partners, stalking, and sexual violence.",
    "Underreporting, racial misclassification, bias, and lack of resources are challenges in solving MMIP cases."
  ]
}
```
